items_en: 1. Commends the Special Committee to Investigate Israeli Practices Affecting the Human Rights of the Population of the Occupied Territories for its efforts to carry out the tasks entrusted to it by the General Assembly and for the objectivity shown by the Special Committee;
2. Regrets that Israel still refuses to allow the Special Committee to visit the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusalem, and other Arab territories occupied by Israel since 1967;
3. Demands that Israel allow the Special Committee to visit the occupied territories;

4. Reaffirms that the occupation itself constitutes a grave violation of the human rights of the Palestinians in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusalem, and other Arab territories occupied by Israel since 1967;

5. Condemns the continued and persistent violation by Israel of the Geneva Convention relative to the Protection of Civilian Persons in Time of War, of 12 August 1949, and other applicable international instruments, and in particular condemns violations of its provisions by the Convention as "serious offences";
6. Declares once again that the serious offences committed by Israel in connection with the said Convention constitute a cry of war and an affront to humanity;

7. Reaffirms, in accordance with the Convention, that the Israeli military occupation of the Palestinian territory, including Jerusalem, and other Arab territories is porary and therefore gives absolutely no right to the occupying Power over the territorial integrity of the occupied territories;
8. Strongly condemns the following Israeli policies and practices:
(a) Annexation of certain parts of occupied Palestinian territory, including Jerusalem;
(b) The subjection of the Syrian Arab Golan to Israeli laws, courts and administration, leading to the de facto anxiety of that territory;
(c) Illegal taxation and collection of taxes and duties;

(d) Establishment of new Israeli settlements and extension of existing settlements on Palestinian lands and other Arab lands, both private and public, and transfer of a foreign population to those settlements;
(e) Eviction, deportation, expulsion, displacement and transfer of Palestinians and other Arabs from these occupied territories and denial of their right to return;

(f) Confiscation and expropriation of private and public property of Palestinians and other Arabs in these occupied territories and any other transactions involving the acquisition of land by Israeli authorities, institutions or residents;
(g) Excavation and transformation of the landscape and historical, cultural and religious sites, in particular in Jรฉru-Salem;
(h) Tracing of archaeological and cultural heritage;
(1) Destruction and demolition of Palestinian and other Arab houses;
(1) Collective punishment, mass arrests, administrative detention and abuse of Palestinians and other Arabs;
(k) Torture of Palestinians and other Arabs;
[) Obstacles to religious and religious freedoms and practices and to respect for family rights and customs;
(m) Obstacles to education and to the economic and social development and health treatment of Palestinians and other Arabs living in these occupied territories;
(n) Obstacles to the right to freedom of movement within the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusa Lem, and other Arab territories occupied by Israel from then on 1967;
(o) Illegal exploitation of the natural wealth, resources and labour of these occupied territories;
9. Strongly condemns, in particular, the following Israeli policies and practices:
(a) Subjection of the Palestinian people, within the occupied Palestinian territory, to the policy of the iron grip;
(b) The escalation of Israeli brutality since the end of the uprising (intifada) on 9 December 1987;
(c) Abuse and torture of children and minors detained or imprisoned;
(d) Closure of the headquarters and offices of trade unions and social services, harassment, including expulsion, of their leaders and attack on hospitals and hospital staff;

(e) Violations of press freedom, including censorship, detention or expulsion of journalists, closure and suspension of newspapers and periodicals, and interference with the work of the international media;
(f) Defenceless demonstrators killed or injured;
(g) Thousands of civilians to broken members;
(h) Residences, either at home or in a locality;
(t) Use of toxic gases, including the death of many Palestinians;

10. Condemns the Israeli repression against the educational institutions of the occupied Syrian Arab Golan and the closure of these institutions, in particular the interdiction of Syrian textbooks and curricula, the obstacles faced by Syrian students to prevent them from pursuing higher education in Syrian uni-versities, the denial of the right to return to school

Syrians are doing higher education in the Syrian Arab Republic, imposing on Syrian students the hew and courses that promote hatred, prejudice and religious intolerance, the dismissal of teachers, all of which are in flagrant violation of the Geneva Convention;
11. Strongly condemns the arming of Israeli settlers in those occupied territories to enable them to perpetrate acts of violence against Palestinians and other Arabs that cause deaths and injuries;

12. Requests the Security Council to ensure that Israel complies with and complies with all provisions of the Geneva Convention relative to the Protection of Civilian Persons in Time of War, of 12 August 1949, in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusalem, and in other Arab territories occupied by Israel since 196 7, and to take measures to put an end to Israeli policies and practices in those territories;

13. Urges the Security Council to consider the current situation in the Palestinian territory occupied by Israel since 1967, taking into account the recommendations contained in the reports of the Secretary-General, with a view to ensuring international protection for the defenceless Palestinian people until Israel, the occupying Power, withdraws from that territory;

14. Reaffirms that all measures taken by Israel to change the physical character, demographic composition, institutional structure or legal status of those occupied territories or any part thereof, including Jerusalem, are null and void and that Israel, by installing part of its population and new immigrants in those occupied territories, is guilty of a flagrant violation of the Geneva Convention and the relevant resolutions of the United Nations;
15. Demands that Israel immediately renounce the policies and practices referred to in paragraphs 8, 9, 10 and 11 above;

16. Calls upon Israel, the occupying Power, to provide for the reopening of the Roman Catholic hospital-hospice in Jerusalem, to ensure that the medical and health care needs of the Palestinians in the city continue to be impaired;

17. Also calls upon Israel, the occupying Power, to take immediate measures to ensure that, in accordance with Security Council resolution 237 (1967) of 14 June 1967, all displaced Arabs and Palestinians return to their homes or former places of residence in the territories occupied by Israel since 1967;

18. Urges international organizations, including the specialized agencies, in particular the International Labour Organization, the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization and the World Health Organization, to continue to review the situation in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusalem, and other Arab territories occupied by Israel since 1967;

1 Y. Reiterates its call upon all States, in particular the States parties to the Geneva Convention, under article 1 of the Convention, as well as international organizations, including specialized institutions, not to recognize any changes made by Israel, the occupying Power, in those occupied territories and to avoid taking measures, including assistance, that Israel could use to pursue its annexation and colonization policy or any other policy or practice referred to in this resolution;

20. Requests the Special Committee, pending the imminent end of the Israeli occupation, to continue to investigate Israeli policies and practices in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusalem, and other Arab territories occupied by Israel since 196, 7/ to consult with the International Committee of the Red Cross in order to safeguard the well-being and human rights of the peoples of those occupied territories and to submit a report to the Secretary-General as soon as possible and thereafter whenever necessary;
21. Also requests the Special Committee to submit to the Secretary-General periodic reports on the situation in the Occupied Palestinian Territory;

22. Further requests the Special Committee to continue to pursue the treatment of prisoners in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including Jerusalem, and other Arab territories occupied by Israel since 1967;

23. Condemns Israel ' s refusal to allow persons from the Occupied Palestinian Territory to appear as witnesses before the Special Committee and to participate in conferences and meetings held outside that territory;
24. Requests the Secretary-General:

To provide the Special Committee with all the means necessary for it to investigate the Israeli policies and practices referred to in this resolution, including the means necessary for the Committee to visit those occupied territories;
To continue to provide the Special Committee with the additional staff it may need to fulfil its tasks;
(c) To transmit regularly to the States Members of the Organization the periodic reports referred to in paragraph 21 above;

(d) To ensure the widest possible dissemination to the Special Committee's ports and its activities and conclusions by all means available to the Department of Public Information of the Secretariat and, if necessary, to reprint the reports of the Special Committee that are exhausted;
(e) To submit a report to the General Assembly at its forty-fifth session on the performance of the tasks entrusted to it under the present resolution;

25. Decides that the Special Committee shall be known as: "Special Committee to Investigate Israeli Practices Affecting the Human Rights of the Palestinian People and Other Arabs of the Occupied Territories

26. Decides also to include in the agenda of its forty-fifth session the item entitled "Report of the Special Committee to Investigate Israeli Practices Affecting the Human Rights of the Palestinian People and Other Arabs of the Occupying Territories".

items_fr: 1. Exige  une fo is  de plus qu'Israรซl,  Puissance  occuยญ pante,  cesse  immรฉdiatement de  prendre  aucune  mesure qui aurait pour effet de modifier le statut juridique, le caยญ ractรจre gรฉographique ou la  composition dรฉmographique du territoire palestinien et des autres territoires arabes ocยญ cupรฉs depuis  1967,  y compris Jรฉrusalem;

items_en: 1. Recognizes that all such measures and decisions taken by Israel in the Palestinian and other Arab territories occupied since 1967, including Jerusalem, violate the relevant provisions of the Geneva Convention relative to the Protection of Civilian Persons in Time of War, of 12 August 1949, constitute a serious obstacle to efforts to establish a just and lasting overall peace in the Middle East and therefore have no legal value;

2. Strongly regrets that Israel continues to implement these measures, in particular the establishment of settlements in the Palestinian territory and other occupied Arab territories, including Jerusalem;

items_en: 1. Takes note of the report of the Special Committee on Peacekeeping Operations;

Also encourages Member States to establish national training programmes for military and civilian personnel in peacekeeping operations and, in this regard, requests the Secretary-General to prepare training manuals for Member States to draw up guidelines for their national or regional training programmes;
7. Stresses the need to ensure a safe and sound financial base for United Nations peacekeeping operations;

8. Urges all Member States to accept their assessed contributions in full and on time, and also encourages Member States that have the capacity to do so to make voluntary contributions that are consistent with the approval of the Secretary-General;

9. Urges all countries hosting a United Nations peacekeeping operation and all parties directly concerned to extend all possible support to such operations in order to facilitate their deployment and operation;

10. Considers that status-of-forces agreements should be concluded between the host countries of a United Nations peacekeeping operation and the latter and, to this end, calls upon those countries to conclude such agreements with the United Nations as soon as possible after the establishment of the operation;

11. Requests the Secretary-General to establish a model agreement on the status of forces between the United Nations and countries hosting peacekeeping operations, while providing the necessary flexibility to take into account the diversity of such operations, and to communicate this model agreement to Member States;
